mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity utilization peaks that precede supply bottlenecks and inflation shocks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of late-cycle periods with rising INDPRO, or the mechanism fails to separate from baseline market trends in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, which systematically reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 20-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or the Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent quarter-over-quarter, strong macroeconomic momentum lifts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable federal funds rates signals a soft landing environment that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market corrections, sustained declines in housing starts signal weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of housing correction periods, or the signal fails to maintain directional accuracy in regime-specific out-of-sample windows.
